title: Disabling CSRF protections in Spring applications
description: A cross-site request forgery (CSRF) attack occurs when a trusted user of a web application can be forced, by an attacker, to perform sensitive actions that he didn't intend. Spring Security provides by default a protection against CSRF attacks which can be disabled.
recommendation: Do not disable Spring Security CSRF protection.

checks.message: Rule: Disabling CSRF protections in Spring applications
